inputs.prompt: Speaker: James Chen, CEO, Clearwater Analytics. Occasion: Press launch of the AI budget forecasting tool, Manchester Town Hall. Audience: Journalists, local government finance professionals, technology press. Key messages: 1) Public money deserves the same analytical tools as private money. 2) This tool was built with and for local government, not adapted from the private sector. Length: 5 minutes. Register: Confident, direct, accessible to non-technical audience.

Speaker: Helen Cross, Director, Riverside Arts Centre. Occasion: Re-opening after renovation. Audience: Community members, donors, local press. Key messages: 1) This building belongs to the community. 2) Thank the lottery fund and volunteers. Length: 3 minutes. Register: Warm, community-focused.

Speaker: Dr. Ingrid Nørgaard, Executive Director, Nordic Health Initiative. Occasion: EU Health Summit plenary session, Brussels. Audience: 400 delegates — health ministers, EU officials, researchers, healthcare industry. Key messages: 1) Cross-border research works and we have 17 years of evidence. 2) The EU must treat health research as infrastructure, not discretionary spending. 3) The Baltic expansion proves the model is scalable beyond Scandinavia. Length: 8 minutes. Register: Authoritative, direct, optimistic but evidence-grounded. The speaker is experienced on stage.

Speaker: Michael Torres, brother of the deceased. Occasion: Memorial service for Elena Torres, a war correspondent killed while reporting in a conflict zone. She was 41. Audience: 200 attendees — family, colleagues from multiple news organisations, journalism students she mentored. Key messages: 1) She was my sister before she was anyone's hero. 2) She was funny, stubborn, and terrible at answering texts. 3) The work she did mattered, and the price she paid was not fair. Length: 6 minutes. Register: Personal, honest, occasionally funny — she would have hated a solemn tribute. Note: This is a eulogy, not an obituary. The speaker will be emotional. Include natural pause points.

Speaker: Catherine Lewis, CEO, Greenleaf Manufacturing. Occasion: All-hands meeting announcing facility closure and 800 job losses. Audience: 1,200 employees, many of whom have worked at the facility for 15+ years. Key messages: 1) This decision was not made lightly. 2) Every affected employee will receive severance, retraining support, and job placement assistance. 3) The closure is driven by market conditions, not performance — the workforce has been exceptional. Length: 10 minutes. Register: Direct, empathetic, no corporate euphemisms. Do not say 'right-sizing' or 'optimising.' The employees deserve honesty. Note: Some employees have already heard rumours. There may be hostile questions afterward.

Speaker: Dr. Amina Okafor, Chair of the African Union Commission on Digital Infrastructure. Occasion: Opening address at the Pan-African Digital Summit, Kigali. Audience: 600 delegates — heads of state, tech executives, international development partners, African tech founders. Key messages: 1) Africa will not repeat the extractive infrastructure model — our digital infrastructure must be owned, governed, and operated by Africans. 2) The 14 data sovereignty agreements signed this year prove the model works. 3) We welcome partnerships, not dependency. Length: 12 minutes. Register: Stateswoman addressing peers and partners — authoritative, visionary, warmly firm. The audience includes both allies and parties whose interests may conflict with the message.
